data.title: MongoDB makes strong start to FY27; analysts identify AI upside
data.description:
MongoDB's (MDB) first-quarter results and guidance produced a strong start to fiscal 2027, prompting an array of financial firms to increase their price targets on the stock.

During early post-market action on Thursday, MongoDB's shares shot up more than 20%. However, by early market action on Friday, the cloud database company's shares were down 2%.

"MDB raised its FY27 guidance, which was well above Street estimates across the board as the company looks to capitalize on demand to modernize legacy applications while remaining conservative around Atlas growth into Q4, said Wedbush analysts, led by Dan Ives, in a Friday investor report. "FY27 total revenue is estimated to be in the range of $2.92 billion to $2.96 billion, which was above the Street's $2.90 billion estimate, including Atlas revenue growth of 23% y/y–25% y/y vs. prior guidance of 21% y/y–23% y/y as the company remains conservative given the decreased visibility into future quarters."

Wedbush maintained its Outperform rating and increased its price target to $390 from $380.

"This was a much-needed strong quarter by MDB following its FY4Q26 misstep as the company continues adding more customers that are in the early stages of infrastructure modernization while scaling out AI workloads with MDB representing the essential database for AI," Ives added.

Citi Research retained its Buy rating and inched its hefty price target up to $455 from $450.

"The FY27 revenue guide was raised by +$60M, well in excess of the 1Q outperformance (+25M) and modest M&A impact (~$6M), reflecting strong momentum and visibility in Atlas (200 bps raise) and EA," Citi analysts said in a Friday note. "While the Q2 guide is exceptionally strong (5% above consensus and much stronger QoQ Seasonality), EA is a big driver at +20% Y/Y, though we view the Atlas guide of 26% as impressive still given the 3pt tougher compare and continue to see a pathway to 30%+ growth in Q2. Profitability was also raised, showing incremental discipline and AI efficiencies in the model."

Meanwhile, Mizuho Securities increased its price target to $400 from $325, Piper Sandler raised its target to $400 from $330, Stifel hiked its target up to $435 from $330, and Needham bumped its target 33% higher to $400 from $300.

data.title: AST SpaceMobile is the best performing communication services stock in the past month
data.description:
As the month of May comes to an end, below is a list of the top 10 communication services stocks ranked by their past one-month price performance. The list includes market capitalization data and Quant ratings for each stock within the communication services sector.

The list is topped by AST SpaceMobile, Inc. (ASTS), with an impressive one-month performance of 85.16%. Iridium Communications Inc. (IRDM), Rumble Inc. (RUM), and StubHub Holdings, Inc. (STUB) round out the top four performers, each posting gains above 30%.

While many stocks on the list carry Hold ratings, several have earned more favorable assessments. Sirius XM Holdings Inc. (SIRI) and Reddit, Inc. (RDDT) have both received Buy ratings, with RDDT earning a Quant Rating of 4.37. Warner Music Group Corp. (WMG) stands out with a Strong Buy Quant Rating of 4.52, despite ranking tenth in terms of monthly performance.

Here is the list:

  1. AST SpaceMobile, Inc. (ASTS), 1 month performance percentage: 85.16%

  2. Iridium Communications Inc. (IRDM), 1 month performance percentage: 37.24%

  3. Rumble Inc. (RUM), 1 month performance percentage: 34.95%

  4. StubHub Holdings, Inc. (STUB), 1 month performance percentage: 31.19%

  5. Lumen Technologies, Inc. (LUMN), 1 month performance percentage: 22.71%

  6. Roku, Inc. (ROKU), 1 month performance percentage: 16.75%

  7. Lionsgate Studios Corp. (LION), 1 month performance percentage: 16.69%

  8. Sirius XM Holdings Inc. (SIRI), 1 month performance percentage: 13.75%

  9. Reddit, Inc. (RDDT), 1 month performance percentage: 13.36%

  10. Warner Music Group Corp. (WMG), 1 month performance percentage: 13.31%

Communication Services ETFs: (XLC), (VOX), (IYZ), (RSPC), and (XTL)

data.title: Jefferies hit by investor suit over water company fraud - report
data.description:
A group of investors sued Jefferies Financial Group (JEF) over bond purchases made by the company's 352 Capital fund in a water vending machine company that federal prosecutors have claimed is a scam, according to a media report.

The complaint was filed in a New York state court, Bloomberg News reported. 352 Capital is part of Jefferies' (JEF) asset management arm, Leucadia Asset Management. The complaint alleges that the fund invested millions of dollars into bonds issued by Water Station Management, which the government and Jefferies have alleged was a fraud, the article said.

The investor class action suit alleges that Leucadia approved the bond purchases even though it knew the underlying collateral didn't exist and was aware of portfolio manager Jordan Chirico's personal investment in the water machine business.

Jefferies (JEF), itself, is suing Chirico, a former 352 portfolio manager. He was charged with fraud by federal prosecutors in New York in August 2025. His lawyer told Bloomberg that Chirico is a “victim” of the alleged fraud. Ryan Wear, the owner of the Water Station business, was also charged with fraud. Both pleaded not guilty.

Another investor, Indiana Public Retirement System, had previously sued Jefferies (JEF) and Leucadia over losses from Water Station, Bloomberg said.

data.title: AST SpaceMobile sinks 18%, leading space stocks lower after Blue Origin rocket explosion
data.description:
Space technology stocks tumble in early trading Friday after a rocket being tested by Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin exploded in a massive fireball on a launchpad at Florida's Cape Canaveral.

Potentially adding to the gloom was a Bloomberg report saying that SpaceX (SPCX) is targeting a valuation of $1.8T, down from a $2T valuation reported in April.

AST SpaceMobile (ASTS), which uses Blue Origin to place communications satellites in space, down 18.5% in early trading.

Deutsche Bank downgrades AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) to Hold from Buy with a $106 price target following the New Glenn rocket explosion, saying ASTS will be unable to meet its launch target of ~45 satellites in orbit by the end of 2026 without Blue Origin; the bank had been estimating that 26 ASTS satellites would be launched via New Glenn this year.

"It's difficult to gauge at this time just how delayed ASTS launch plans will be, but we think it would be very optimistic to expect any New Glenn launches in the next three months and unrealistic that ASTS would have access to the four New Glenn launches we had been estimating for 2026," Deutsche Bank says in a note.

While New Glenn could be back to the launchpad within six months, there will inevitably be fewer launches this year spread across Blue Origin's major customers, including Amazon (AMZN) and the U.S. government, the bank says.

Shares of other U.S. space-related stocks—which have been running hot ahead of the massive SpaceX IPO—trade sharply lower, including Rocket Lab (RKLB) down 5.4%, Intuitive Machines (LUNR) down 12.3%, Redwire (RDW) down 9.6%, Planet Labs (PL) down 9.2%, Voyager Technologies (VOYG) down 9.3%, Karman Holdings (KRMN) down 8%, Firefly Aerospace (FLY) down 5.3%, Satellogic (SATL) down 6.5%, Momentus (MNTS) down 9.3%, MDA Space (MDA) down 7.5%, Sidus Space (SIDU) down 12.4%, York Space Systems (YSS) down 10.1%, Echostar (SATS) down 4.8%, Blacksky Technology (BKSY) down 11.6%.

ETFs: (NASA), (UFO), (ARKX), (ROKT), (JEDI)

data.title: FDA to cut animal testing in cancer drug development
data.description:
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration on Friday proposed to reduce animal studies conducted to assess safety in the development of certain cancer medications, marking the agency’s latest effort to expedite pharmacological development.

The regulator recommended conducting only rodent trials in the nonclinical phase of clinical development or replacing three-month non-human primate studies with evidence-based risk assessment.

The draft guidance, which is available for public comments until July 30, also included recommendations on when to conduct animal trials and other proposals for general toxicology studies.

The FDA identified the move as part of a broader initiative to accelerate drug development, noting that R&D timelines are estimated to currently extend 10–12 years, from discovery to commercialization.

“This draft guidance not only supports the FDA’s commitment to expedite regulatory pathways for meaningful treatments but also fulfills the agency’s promise to reduce the use of animal testing during drug development,” said the FDA's oncology head, Angelo de Claro.

Leading cancer drugmakers include Pfizer (PFE), Merck (MRK), Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY), AstraZeneca (AZN), GSK (GSK), Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), and Novartis (NVS).

Notable contract research organizations exposed to preclinical animal testing: Charles River Laboratories (CRL) and Inotiv (NOTV)

data.title: Judge temporarily blocks Trump’s $1.8B DOJ ‘lawfare’ fund - report
data.description:
A federal judge in Virginia on Friday temporarily blocked the U.S. Department of Justice from taking any additional action on its Anti-Weaponization Fund as legal challenges proceed, according to a media report on Friday.

Judge Leonie Brinkema’s order prohibits the DOJ from transferring money to the fund, considering claims, or disbursing any funds while the lawsuit moves forward, CNBC reported.

The DOJ said earlier this month it was creating the $1.8B fund as part of settling President Donald Trump’s $10B lawsuit against the Internal Revenue Service over the leak of his tax records by an IRS employee.

The fund is intended to compensate individuals who claim they were victims of prosecutorial overreach during the Biden administration. Critics have labeled it a “slush fund” for Trump allies, including participants in the January 6, 2021, Capitol riot.

Judge Brinkema set a June 12 hearing on whether to maintain the block, ordering the Trump administration to file its opposition by June 5, CNBC reported. This lawsuit is one of three federal cases challenging the fund.

data.title: Scotiabank to acquire MapleMark, accessing FDIC insurance for clients
data.description:
The Bank of Nova Scotia (BNS), known as Scotiabank, agreed to acquire Maple Financial Holdings, the parent of MapleMark Bank, giving it FDIC deposit insurance for its clients, the company said Friday. Moreover, the transaction will expand its presence in the U.S.

Gaining Federal Deposit Insurance Corp. protection is important for Scotiabank's mortgage capital markets business and its deposit growth strategy, Travis Machen, CEO and group head of Scotiabank's Global Banking and Markets, said. "MapleMark Bank is a well-run bank primarily operating in Dallas, Texas, and further supports our strategic focus within the North American corridor," he said.

The acquisition isn't expected to have a material impact on Scotiabank's earnings or CET1 ratio, it said.

Scotiabank (BNS) stock slipped 0.3% in morning trading.

data.title: PagerDuty outlines FY2027 EPS of $1.27 to $1.32 while expanding Operations Cloud usage-based pricing
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: PagerDuty (PD) Q1 FY2027

Management View

  • "In Q1, PagerDuty delivered results that exceeded the top end of guidance for both revenue and non-GAAP operating margin." (Executive Chair of the Board Jennifer Tejada)

  • "Quarterly revenue was $121 million, up 1% year-over-year, and annual recurring revenue was $496 million, flat year-over-year." (Executive Chair Tejada)

  • "We grew non-GAAP operating margin to 25% through consistent discipline, structural efficiency initiatives and AI adoption." (Executive Chair Tejada)

  • "Usage-based products, which include AIOps, PagerDuty Advance and Operations Cloud, now account for nearly 10% of our total ARR." (Executive Chair Tejada)

  • "The ARR of customers on this model nearly doubled from Q4 to Q1." (CFO & Principal Financial Officer Howard Wilson)

  • "After 10 years as CEO, I've transitioned to Executive Chair." (Executive Chair Tejada) "I'm pleased to introduce PagerDuty's new CEO, John DiLullo." (Executive Chair Tejada)

  • "In the near term, my priority is simple: listen, learn and engage." (CEO & Director John DiLullo)

Outlook

  • "For the second quarter fiscal 2027, we expect revenue in the range of $122 million to $124 million" and "net income per diluted share attributable to PagerDuty, Inc. in the range of $0.29 to $0.31." (CFO Wilson)

  • "This implies an operating margin of 22% to 23%." (CFO Wilson)

  • "For the full fiscal year 2027, we expect revenue in the range of $488.5 million to $496.5 million" and "net income per diluted share attributable to PagerDuty, Inc. in the range of $1.27 to $1.32." (CFO Wilson)

  • "This is the same range as previously provided." (CFO Wilson) "[EPS is] an increase based on the reduced share count as a result of the completion of the buyback program." (CFO Wilson)

Financial Results

  • "In the first quarter of FY '27... Revenue for the quarter was $121 million" and "Q1 gross margin was 86%" while "operating income was $30 million or 25% of revenue." (CFO Wilson)

  • "GAAP net income was $10.2 million, our fourth consecutive quarter of GAAP profitability." (CFO Wilson)

  • "Dollar-based net retention was 97%." (CFO Wilson) "We expect our continued customer success and renewal initiatives, along with our Operations Cloud pricing to result in stabilization of DBNR and for it to gradually increase throughout the year." (CFO Wilson)

  • "Cash from operations was $44 million" and "free cash flow was $41 million." (CFO Wilson)

  • "We ended the quarter with $444 million in cash, cash equivalents and investments." (CFO Wilson) "At the end of Q1, total RPO was approximately $441 million." (CFO Wilson)

  • "During the quarter, we repurchased 8.5 million shares for $63 million and completed the authorized $200 million share repurchase program." (CFO Wilson) "Today, we have announced our latest $100 million share repurchase program." (CFO Wilson)

Q&A

  • Christopher Quintero, Morgan Stanley, Research Division: "the net retention rate did take a step down"—what drives confidence in stabilization? Executive Chair Tejada: "we have really started to see good progress with our early cohort in the pricing transition" and "we're seeing very strong demand signals" including "our fifth consecutive quarter of over 600 new customer logos." CFO Wilson: "we monitor closely the growth in usage on our platform" and "we saw from Q4 to Q1, nearly doubling the ARR of customers who were on that model."

  • Christopher Quintero, Morgan Stanley, Research Division: what are CEO priorities? CEO DiLullo: "It's really early days for me" and "AI continues to accelerate the creation of growth" and "more software, more dependencies, more automation means more inference, more compute" which "lead[s] to just greater need for resilience and orchestration and real-time operational response."

  • Jonathan Ngo, Truist Securities, Inc., Research Division: balancing "heavy R&D" for agents with margin expansion? Executive Chair Tejada: "We have been building AI into the platform for several years" and "we're able to do that on a lower cost basis than we have in the past." CFO Wilson: "we've always taken a balanced view on capital allocation" and "the one strength that we have... is our gross margins."

  • Andrew Sherman, TD Cowen, Research Division: where can consumption ARR mix go by year-end? CFO Wilson: "We haven't put a specific number out there" and "our expectation is that we will make meaningful progress with this each period as we go through the year."

  • Andrew Sherman, TD Cowen, Research Division: update to AI-native ARR mix? Executive Chair Tejada: "some of these native AI companies are scaling really fast" and "we're seeing the appetite for resilience at scale coming much earlier" while "the most demanding developers are choosing the best-in-breed offering early" through "our PLG motion."

Sentiment Analysis

  • "the net retention rate did take a step down" and questions on margin durability signaled slightly negative to neutral analyst tone, centered on retention mechanics, pricing migration pacing, and investment needs. (Christopher Quintero, Morgan Stanley, Research Division; Jonathan Ngo, Truist Securities, Inc., Research Division; Andrew Sherman, TD Cowen, Research Division)

  • Management tone was slightly positive in prepared remarks and more assertive in Q&A, leaning on confidence markers including "We are confident" (Executive Chair Tejada) and emphasizing early traction: "The ARR of customers on this model nearly doubled" (CFO Wilson).

  • Versus last quarter, the call added leadership-transition uncertainty but maintained similar messaging on consumption pricing and AI as a driver; Q&A focus remained anchored on retention and the pricing shift (Q4: "seat-based compression"; Q1: "mitigate some of the pressure around gross retention because of seat-based compression"). (Executive Chair Tejada; CFO Wilson)

Quarter-over-quarter Comparison

  • "After 10 years as CEO, I've transitioned to Executive Chair" marked a leadership change versus Q4, when Tejada spoke as "Chair of the Board & CEO." (Executive Chair Tejada)

  • Q1 emphasized Operations Cloud early traction in quantified terms: "The ARR of customers on this model nearly doubled from Q4 to Q1," versus Q4 framing that "we anticipate by the end of this fiscal year that a meaningful portion of our ARR will be under the new licensing model" without a disclosed migration metric. (CFO Wilson)

  • Guidance posture stayed flat on FY2027 revenue: Q1 said "This is the same range as previously provided," while raising EPS range due to "reduced share count" after completing the buyback program and launching a new authorization. (CFO Wilson)

  • Analyst focus shifted from Q4 go-to-market execution and pricing receptivity to Q1 scrutiny on "net retention" stabilization and the practical pace of consumption expansion within ARR. (Christopher Quintero, Morgan Stanley, Research Division; Andrew Sherman, TD Cowen, Research Division)

Risks and Concerns

  • "not every customer in our base is exactly the same" and management described parallel pressures from customers "in the transitional phase" alongside expansion opportunities tied to usage-based pricing. (CFO Wilson)

  • "Our customer base is also in transition" and "we have some segments of the customer base that are under more financial pressure than others, including like midsized SaaS." (Executive Chair Tejada)

  • "Q1 free cash flow was elevated primarily due to overperformance on collections, which we expect to normalize in Q2" and "part of the Q1 overperformance was due to marketing program spend, which we expect to deploy in Q2." (CFO Wilson)

Final Takeaway

PagerDuty’s management positioned the quarter around a CEO transition, a usage-based business model shift, and sustained margin discipline, highlighting that Operations Cloud pricing delivered early traction (including nearly doubled ARR among customers on the model from Q4 to Q1) while reiterating flat full-year revenue guidance and lifting full-year EPS guidance tied to share repurchases and a reduced share count.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
